task: Root cause analysis of trade 5230 guardian SL failure
opus_correction: Guardian widens SL from 2.5×ATR to max(3×ATR, E100+0.5×ATR buffer). Trade 5230: SL widened to ~35p. MFE only +3.4p, below _RATCHET_THRESH_PIPS=5.0, so no profit floor locked. Threat scoring too slow for 7-minute reversal. Three fixes: (1) hard max_loss_pips circuit breaker, (2) lower ratchet threshold 5→3p, (3) time-based exit for non-performing trades. Fix 1 most impactful — caps damage even when guardian's internal systems fail.
opus_reasoning: Traced exact execution path: entry → SL widening (lines 1400-1489) → ratchet check (threshold too high) → threat scoring lag → catastrophic exit. Each protection addresses a different failure point in the chain. Ranked by impact: circuit breaker prevents worst case regardless of other failures.

task: Expose guardian hardcoded constants as optimizer parameters
local_output: Guardian has 3 hardcoded constants that should be tunable
opus_correction: Three params for tuning_config.py: guardian.ratchet_activation_pips (range 1.0-8.0, currently 5.0), guardian.max_loss_pips (range 15-40, new circuit breaker), guardian.sl_widen_atr_mult (range 2.0-4.0, currently 3.0). Wire into candle-walk replay for Bayesian search. 196 trades of history available for optimization. The 5.0p threshold was set by gut feel on April 2nd — optimizer can find the actual optimal value.
opus_reasoning: Identified that all three constants were hardcoded rather than tunable. Connected to existing Bayesian optimizer infrastructure (tuning_config.py + replay.py candle-walk). Each param has a clear range based on domain knowledge: ratchet must be >0 but <typical MFE, max_loss must exceed normal SL but cap catastrophic, widen mult must give room but not too much.

task: Audit SL-killed trades for structural SL improvement
local_output: 31 losing trades last week, need to quantify structural SL impact
opus_correction: Systematic audit: 31 losers → 10 SL-killed (OANDA stop fired before guardian) → fetched M15 candles → computed EMA-aware structural SL per trade. 9/10 saved (171.8 pips, $798.91). Structural SL (E100+buffer) won 6/9 — those pairs had E100 far enough from entry. ATR-3x won remaining 3 — those had stale 1.5x multiplier from core.db early in week. One trade (#2795) not saved — exit landed exactly at 3×ATR level. 19 guardian-managed exits unaffected (guardian closed before hard SL).
opus_reasoning: Methodical approach: filter SL-killed from total losers, fetch actual candle data to compute real EMA positions, compare two SL methods (structural vs ATR-based) per trade. The structural vs ATR-3x comparison reveals that the optimal SL depends on pair-specific EMA spacing, not a universal multiplier.
